explanation:
**Pregnancy and family planning** involve health, finances, and education. Each stage has specific milestones, expenses, and decisions.

## How it works

This calculator computes expected weight gain based on your pre-pregnancy BMI using the 2009 IOM guidelines. It checks your values against typical ranges and alerts you if results fall outside normal parameters.

## Important notes

This calculator is for informational purposes only. For critical medical, financial, or legal decisions, consult a qualified healthcare professional. Values and guidelines are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much weight should I gain during pregnancy?
faq.a: It depends on your pre-pregnancy BMI. Normal BMI (18.5–24.9): 11–15 kg. Underweight (<18.5): 12.5–18 kg. Overweight (25–29.9): 6–11 kg. Obese (≥30): 5–9 kg.

faq.q: What's healthy pregnancy weight gain per week?
faq.a: First trimester: 0.5–1 kg total. Second and third trimesters: 0.3–0.5 kg per week.
Your pre-pregnancy BMI determines recommended weight gain to support fetal development while minimizing pregnancy complications and postpartum weight retention.

faq.q: What if I'm having twins or multiples?
faq.a: Twin pregnancy typically requires 16–25 kg total weight gain, depending on pre-pregnancy weight. Consult your OB-GYN for personalized guidance.

faq.q: When should I be concerned about weight changes?
faq.a: Contact your doctor if you experience unexplained weight loss or gain more than 2 kg in a single week, as this may indicate gestational issues.

faq.q: Is it normal to lose weight in early pregnancy?
faq.a: Slight weight loss in the first trimester due to nausea is common. Significant or unexplained loss warrants medical evaluation.

faq.q: Can I diet or restrict calories while pregnant?
faq.a: No. Focus on nutrient-dense foods and consistent calorie intake. Crash diets or severe calorie restriction can harm fetal development. Work with a healthcare provider or dietitian.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are free and require no account or registration.
